task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Full SaaS: Project Tracker** (Level 10)

Build a project management API. Users register/login. Projects have name, description, status (active/archived). Tasks belong to projects with title, description, status (todo/in_progress/done), assignee_id. Auth required. Users can only see projects they own. Full CRUD on both resources.

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for javascript backend

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique, email
  password, required
  name
final_source:
/*
SaaS Project Tracker (Level 10)
Full CRUD auth-protected API with JWT tokens
*/

build for web and javascript backend

database is local memory

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique, email
  password, required
  name, required

create a Projects table:
  name, required
  description
  status
  owner_id, required

create a Tasks table:
  title, required
  description
  status
  project_id, required
  assignee_id

# Registration
when user calls POST /api/register sending creds:
  validate creds:
    email is text, required, matches email
    password is text, required
    name is text, required
  saved = save creds to Users
  send back saved with status 201

# Login
when user calls POST /api/login sending creds:
  validate creds:
    email is text, required, matches email
    password is text, required
  user = look up User with creds's email
  guard user is not null or 'User not found'
  guard user's password is creds's password or 'Password incorrect'
  send back { token is 'token_' + user's id } with status 200

# Projects CRUD
when user calls POST /api/projects sending data:
  requires login
  set data's owner_id to caller's id
  saved = save data to Projects
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls GET /api/projects:
  requires login
  projects = get all Projects where owner_id is caller's id
  send back projects

when user calls GET /api/projects/:id:
  requires login
  project = look up Project with this id
  guard project is not null or 'Not found'
  guard project's owner_id is caller's id or 'Forbidden'
  send back project

when user calls PUT /api/projects/:id sending updates:
  requires login
  project = look up Project with this id
  guard project is not null or 'Not found'
  guard project's owner_id is caller's id or 'Forbidden'
  save updates to Projects
  send back 'updated'

when user calls DELETE /api/projects/:id:
  requires login
  project = look up Project with this id
  guard project is not null or 'Not found'
  guard project's owner_id is caller's id or 'Forbidden'
  delete Project with this id
  send back 'deleted'

# Tasks CRUD
when user calls POST /api/projects/:id/tasks sending data:
  requires login
  project = look up Project with this id
  guard project's owner_id is caller's id or 'Forbidden'
  set data's project_id to this id
  saved = save data to Tasks
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls GET /api/projects/:id/tasks:
  requires login
  project = look up Project with this id
  guard project's owner_id is caller's id or 'Forbidden'
  tasks = get all Tasks where project_id is this id
  send back tasks

when user calls GET /api/tasks/:id:
  requires login
  task = look up Task with this id
  guard task is not null or 'Not found'
  project = look up Project with task's project_id
  guard project's owner_id is caller's id or 'Forbidden'
  send back task

when user calls PUT /api/tasks/:id sending updates:
  requires login
  task = look up Task with this id
  project = look up Project with task's project_id
  guard project's owner_id is caller's id or 'Forbidden'
  save updates to Tasks
  send back 'updated'

when user calls DELETE /api/tasks/:id:
  requires login
  task = look up Task with this id
  project = look up Project with task's project_id
  guard project's owner_id is caller's id or 'Forbidden'
  delete Task with this id
  send back 'deleted'

# Simple frontend
page 'App' at '/':
  heading 'Project Tracker'
  section 'Create':
    'Name' is a text input saved as pname
    'Description' is a text input saved as pdesc
    button 'Create Project':
      send { name: pname, description: pdesc } as a new record to '/api/projects'
  section 'Projects':
    on page load get projects from '/api/projects'
    display projects as table
